pattern: A stack evaluates postfix expressions by pushing operands and, on each operator, popping the two most recent results and pushing the combined value.
hints: In postfix form an operator always applies to the two values immediately before it, which is exactly what a stack remembers.
Scan left to right: push numbers; on an operator pop the right operand then the left operand, compute, and push the result back.
Mind operand order for subtraction and division (left was pushed first), and make division truncate toward zero rather than floor for negatives.

edge_inputs.description: negative division truncates toward zero, not floor

stress.description: long randomly built valid postfix expression kept within 32-bit range
stress.note: avoids division to keep magnitudes bounded and excludes divide-by-zero
